threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when a bus driver skips a stop because they’re too busy chatting with mates at the front. That’s a classic local move.
Saw a guy on the 59 passionately debating with himself about the best Jerk chicken in Brixton. He almost had a full crowd by the end of it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids playing Fußball with a makeshift goal made of old bicycles in the street. That was the moment I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a cafe that doubles as a record store, with vinyl that plays while your coffee brews. It's a vibe that feels uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to a BART delay—eye rolls and a quick check for a backup bus route. Tourists just stand there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard a guy on the 19 Polk start a karaoke rendition of 'I Will Survive'—even a few tourists joined in. That’s the Muni spirit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Plaça del Sol is all about the sun; locals know it’s the shade from those big trees that makes it a proper hangout.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still puzzled by the bells at 8 PM, checking their phones instead of enjoying the plaza vibe.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver talks about 'getting into' the 30 instead of just catching it. Proper bus lingo takes time.
Caught a late 333 last week; a bloke serenading the driver with a guitar. Everyone tried to ignore him, but it was oddly soothing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy in a tutu selling falafel outside the U-Bahn on a rainy Tuesday. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a Späti with an open-air cinema in the back, complete with weird indie films and the scent of döner wafting through.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they navigate around BART delays like a seasoned sailor in fog—straight to the taco truck instead of waiting at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the N-Judah with an accordion at Powell, locals tapping their feet while tourists just looked confused. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but they miss the tiny bars tucked away in the back streets where locals gather for conversations that last all night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their confusion over the Mercat de Gràcia’s layout – locals know the secret shortcuts and hidden gems.
